options.label: Useful
options.description: Directly relevant and core for downstream synthesis. A useful chunk must add distinct value, not just topical similarity. If another chunk already expresses substantially the same meaning more clearly or more completely, the weaker one is usually not useful. If two chunks genuinely complement each other and each adds a new part of the same idea, both may still be useful.

options.label: Borderline
options.description: Relevant or partly relevant, but not core. Borderline includes chunks that are weaker, more indirect, more generic, more partial, or substantially overlapping with a stronger chunk.

options.label: Discarded
options.description: Not relevant enough, too weak, too generic, too repetitive, or not useful for downstream synthesis.
